"cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-625", "name": "Permissive Regular Expression"}], "description": "## Summary

`Rack::Directory` interpolates the configured `root` path directly into a regular expression when deriving the displayed directory path. If `root` contains regex metacharacters such as `+`, `*`, or `.`, the prefix stripping can fail and the generated directory listing may expose the full filesystem path in the HTML output.

## Details

`Rack::Directory::DirectoryBody#each` computes the visible path using code equivalent to:

```ruby
show_path = Utils.escape_html(path.sub(/\A#{root}/, ''))
```

Here, `root` is a developer-configured filesystem path. It is normalized earlier with `File.expand_path(root)` and then inserted directly into a regular expression without escaping.

Because the value is treated as regex syntax rather than as a literal string, metacharacters in the configured path can change how the prefix match behaves. When that happens, the expected root prefix is not removed from `path`, and the absolute filesystem path is rendered into the HTML directory listing.

## Impact

If `Rack::Directory` is configured to serve a directory whose absolute path contains regex metacharacters, the generated directory listing may disclose the full server filesystem path instead of only the request-relative path.

This can expose internal deployment details such as directory layout, usernames, mount points, or naming conventions that would otherwise not be visible to clients.

## Mitigation

* Update to a patched version of Rack in which the root prefix is removed using an escaped regular expression.
* Avoid using `Rack::Directory` with a root path that contains regular expression metacharacters.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0024, "percentile": 0.14915}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-02T20:32:42Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-34763"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-02T17:16:24Z", "published_at": "2026-04-02T20:32:42Z", "references": ["https://github.com/rack/rack/security/advisories/GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-34763", "https://github.com/rubysec/ruby-advisory-db/blob/master/gems/rack/CVE-2026-34763.yml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/rack/rack/security-advisories/GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/rack/rack", "summary": "Rack has a root directory disclosure via unescaped regex interpolation in Rack::Directory", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-13T16:18:23Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-7mqq-6cf9-v2qp", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.2.23", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "rack"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.2.23"}, {"first_patched_version": "3.1.21", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "rack"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.0.0.beta1, < 3.1.21"}, {"first_patched_version": "3.2.6", "package": {"ecosystem": "rubygems", "name": "rack"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.2.0, < 3.2.6"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Rack has a root directory disclosure via unescaped regex interpolation in Rack::Directory
